commands.meaning: Sets the Cisco device hostname in global configuration mode.
use_for: Apply an approved device naming standard
good_output: Prompt changes to the intended hostname
bad_output: Wrong device name or naming-standard conflict
commands.safety_level: Medium - changes device identity in the configuration.
commands.learning_note: Confirm the target switch before changing its name.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE basic system management documentation.

commands.meaning: Creates or enters a VLAN configuration context.
commands.safety_level: Medium - can affect switching when assigned to ports.
commands.learning_note: Creating a VLAN does not place ports into it.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE VLAN configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Assigns a name to the selected Cisco VLAN.
good_output: Name matches the approved VLAN plan
commands.safety_level: Low - documentation change within VLAN configuration.
commands.learning_note: Use clear names that identify service or location.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE VLAN configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Selects multiple Cisco interfaces for a shared configuration.
use_for: Apply an approved identical setting to a known port set
bad_output: Management, uplink, or unrelated ports included

next_commands.why: Verify one or more affected ports.

commands.safety_level: High - applies later commands to multiple interfaces.
commands.learning_note: Check the exact range before entering any configuration command.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE interface configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Configures a Layer 2 interface to operate as a trunk.
use_for: Approved switch-to-switch or switch-to-router VLAN transport
good_output: Expected trunk forms and passes approved VLANs
bad_output: Endpoint port becomes a trunk or VLAN path changes unexpectedly

next_commands.why: Verify operational trunk state and allowed VLANs.

commands.safety_level: High - can alter VLAN reachability.
commands.learning_note: Never trunk an endpoint port without documented design and approval.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE VLAN trunk configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Restricts which VLANs may traverse a Cisco trunk.
commands.safety_level: High - can interrupt VLAN services.
commands.learning_note: Use add/remove syntax carefully to avoid replacing an existing allowed list.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE VLAN trunk configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Assigns a Cisco voice VLAN to an access port for an IP phone.
good_output: Correct data and voice VLANs on phone port
bad_output: Phone loses registration or wrong voice VLAN

next_commands.why: Verify access and voice VLAN settings.

commands.safety_level: Medium - can affect IP phone service.
commands.learning_note: Confirm QoS, CDP/LLDP, and voice VLAN design first.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE voice VLAN configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Adjusts Cisco STP bridge priority to make the switch preferred root for a VLAN.
good_output: Expected root bridge and stable topology
bad_output: Unexpected topology change or root movement

next_commands.why: Verify root bridge and port roles.
next_commands.meaning: Shows STP state for a VLAN.

commands.safety_level: High - changes Layer 2 forwarding topology.
commands.learning_note: Change STP root only with a topology plan and maintenance approval.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE LAN switching configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Sets Cisco switch-stack member election priority.
use_for: Prepare an approved stack master preference
bad_output: Incorrect member selected or unplanned master election
commands.safety_level: High - affects future stack election behavior.
commands.learning_note: Priority normally takes effect on a later election; do not force an election casually.

commands.meaning: Assigns an IPv4 address to a selected Layer 3 or SVI interface.
use_for: Configure an approved management or routed interface
bad_output: Duplicate address or lost management reachability
commands.safety_level: High - can disconnect management or create an IP conflict.
commands.learning_note: Use console or alternate access for management IP changes.
commands.source_note: Cisco IOS XE interface configuration documentation.

commands.meaning: Sets the HP Comware device name in system view.
commands.safety_level: Medium - changes device identity.
commands.learning_note: Confirm the current device before changing sysname.

commands.meaning: Creates or enters an HP Comware VLAN view.
commands.safety_level: Medium - can affect switching once assigned.
commands.learning_note: VLAN creation and port assignment are separate steps.

commands.meaning: Sets a Comware Layer 2 port to trunk mode.
commands.safety_level: High - can change VLAN forwarding.
commands.learning_note: Confirm the neighbor and allowed VLAN design first.

commands.meaning: Allows selected VLANs on a Comware trunk port.
commands.safety_level: High - can interrupt VLAN services.
commands.learning_note: Confirm existing permitted VLANs before replacing an allow-list.

commands.meaning: Sets HP Comware IRF member election priority.
use_for: Prepare an approved IRF master preference
bad_output: Wrong member or unexpected master election
commands.safety_level: High - affects future IRF master elections.
commands.learning_note: Priority change does not itself trigger a master election.
commands.source_note: HPE FlexNetwork Comware virtualization command reference.

commands.meaning: Changes the configured ID of an IRF member.
use_for: Pre-stage a unique member ID during an approved IRF build
commands.safety_level: Critical - requires reboot and can invalidate member-ID related settings.
commands.learning_note: Use only in a controlled build or approved maintenance plan.
commands.source_note: HPE FlexNetwork Comware virtualization command reference.

commands.meaning: Saves current Comware configuration without an interactive confirmation.
use_for: Save an approved and verified configuration
commands.safety_level: High - persists changes across reboot.
commands.learning_note: Review the configuration diff before saving.

commands.meaning: Sets the ArubaOS-CX switch hostname.
good_output: Prompt and running configuration use expected name
commands.safety_level: Medium - changes device identity.
commands.learning_note: Confirm the target device first.

commands.meaning: Creates or enters an ArubaOS-CX VLAN context.
commands.safety_level: Medium - affects switching when assigned.
commands.learning_note: Create, name, assign, and verify in separate deliberate steps.

commands.meaning: Defines allowed VLANs for an ArubaOS-CX trunk interface.
commands.safety_level: High - can interrupt VLAN services.
commands.learning_note: Record the previous allowed list for rollback.

commands.meaning: Sets the native VLAN for an ArubaOS-CX trunk interface.
use_for: Apply an approved native VLAN design
commands.safety_level: High - mismatch can cause VLAN leakage or loss of connectivity.
commands.learning_note: Confirm both ends of the trunk before changing native VLAN.

commands.meaning: Enables active LACP negotiation on an ArubaOS-CX member interface.
use_for: Build an approved LAG member configuration

next_commands.why: Verify member selection and partner state.

commands.safety_level: High - can affect aggregate link forwarding.
commands.learning_note: Configure matching LACP mode and LAG design on both ends.

commands.meaning: Sets ArubaOS-CX system priority used by LACP selection.
use_for: Apply an approved LACP system preference
commands.safety_level: High - can affect LAG negotiation.
commands.learning_note: Understand partner priority and system ID before changing it.

commands.meaning: Sets the ArubaOS-CX bridge priority for spanning-tree election.
commands.safety_level: High - can change Layer 2 forwarding topology.
commands.learning_note: Coordinate STP priority across the switching design.
